intro: Teyleria koordersii, commonly known as the "Java forest fern", is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Teyleria koordersii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
